meaning: n.击昏,击倒adj.击倒的,摧毁性 <美俚> 引人注目,杰出的,迷人的
examples: We are offering odds of 6-1 on the fight ending in a knockout.
Lennox Lewis ended the scheduled 12-round fight with a knockout in the eighth round.
When boxing, do not expect to have the first punch be a knockout.
The latest idea, backed by F1 supremo Bernie Ecclestone, is for 'knockout' qualifying divided into three stages.
The 34-year-old American boxer has won all 42 of his professional fights, 26 by knockout.
